text.title: Django BaseModel Implementation
text.summary: Abstract BaseModel with UUID + timestamps, ForeignKey patterns, SoftDelete extension, and Django 5 features.

section.title: BaseModel

rule.statement: Every domain model must inherit from BaseModel. Never inherit from models.Model directly for domain entities.
rule.rationale: Ensures all models carry uid (UUID), created_at, and updated_at with zero per-model boilerplate.

rule.statement: Set abstract = True in BaseModel.Meta. Omitting it creates a real database table for BaseModel itself.
rule.rationale: An abstract base generates no table; each concrete subclass gets the fields inlined.

section.reference: BaseModel + SoftDeleteModel scaffold with UUIDv7 upgrade path.

example.code:
# core/models.py import uuid from django.db import models
class BaseModel(models.Model): uid = models.UUIDField( default=uuid.uuid4, editable=False, unique=True, db_index=True, ) created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True, db_index=True) updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)
class Meta: abstract = True ordering = ['-created_at'] get_latest_by = 'created_at'
def __str__(self) -> str: return f"{type(self).__name__}({self.uid})"

rule.statement: Choose on_delete deliberately: PROTECT for user/account references, CASCADE only when child rows have no meaning without parent, SET_NULL for optional assignees.
rule.rationale: CASCADE on user references causes silent data loss when an account is deleted. PROTECT forces explicit handling of the relationship.

rule.statement: Always set explicit related_name on ForeignKey fields.
rule.rationale: Auto-generated model_set names break when models are renamed and confuse reverse lookups.

rule.statement: Use SoftDeleteModel only for non-PII data. For personal data subject to deletion requests, use hard delete.
rule.rationale: Soft delete preserves data that users may have a legal right to have erased (GDPR Article 17).

rule.statement: Use db_default for values computed by the database at INSERT time. Use GeneratedField for columns computed from other columns. Pin PostgreSQL when using GeneratedField(db_persist=True) — SQLite support is limited.
rule.rationale: db_default avoids Python-side time skew in distributed systems. GeneratedField keeps computed data consistent without triggers.

rule.statement: Set lookup_field = 'uid' on DRF viewsets. Exclude 'id' from all serializer Meta.fields. Mark uid as read_only in serializers.
rule.rationale: Without explicit lookup_field, DRF defaults to PK lookup and exposes the integer id in URLs despite all the model-level effort.
